meta.engineer_notes: Read /tmp/repomix-app.xml for full file contents. This XML is a condensed handoff for a superintelligent agent to pick up work immediately.

project_overview.goal: Cinematic React SPA (Vite + TS + Tailwind) showcasing Pragma Smart Parking platform. 10-section dark-mode landing page with Three.js globe, GSAP ScrollTrigger, framer-motion, recharts. Live Render backend integration with graceful fallback for cold starts.
project_overview.tech_stack: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 6, Tailwind 3.4, Three.js (@react-three/fiber/drei), Recharts, framer-motion, GSAP
project_overview.backend: FastAPI + Python 3.12 deployed on Render free tier (cold start 30s–10min)

file.purpose: Entry point — renders App with StrictMode and WarmupProvider wrap
Root component — imports all 10 sections, wraps in WarmupProvider
Vite config with proxy (/api→Render) and base path
Data fetching hooks — useApi, useApiWithFallback, usePoll. THE core data layer.
API client — all endpoint functions, JWT injection, retries, timeouts
Hardcoded fallback for every endpoint — renders immediately while API warms
TypeScript types for all API responses
Shared backend state — health poll, auth, markLive, backendReady signal
Non-blocking overlay showing warmup progress, dismissible
Hero with Three.js globe, MetricTicker, dashboard data
3D globe with 800 particles, spatial grid, IntersectionObserver
Animated stat pills (accuracy, cities, slots, revenue)
ML showcase — occupancy chart with Recharts, live API data
MARL pricing — 24×7 heatmap, zone data, stats
Blockchain viz — block cards, chain status, badges
Digital twin scenarios — 6 scenario cards with Run buttons
40-slot grid with state colors, probabilities, category filters
Scrolling terminal log with pause/resume, 5 log types
5-layer architecture diagram (User→Prediction→Pricing→Blockchain→IoT)
4 rotating testimonials with auto-advance
Footer with vision statement, stack, links

section.name: Hero
section.notes: Globe with 800 particles. MetricTicker shows computed values from lots + dashboard.

section.name: Prediction Engine
section.notes: Recharts line+area chart. mapToChart uses occupancy_rate directly.

section.name: Revenue Intelligence
section.data: PricingZone[] → 24×7 heatmap, stats (peak multiplier, revenue lift, latency)
section.notes: API returns single zone object; client.ts wraps it in array for component compatibility.

section.name: Blockchain Ledger
section.data: BlockChainStatus → 5 sample blocks with live chain_length override
section.notes: chain_valid→valid mapping in client.ts. Blocks are hardcoded samples with live index overlay.

section.name: Digital Twin
section.data: Scenario[] → 6 cards (heavy rain, city event, earthquake, holiday, emergency, festival)
section.notes: API endpoint may exist but its fetch may be idle. Falls back to fallbackScenarios. Run buttons fire runScenario POST.

section.name: Micro-Slot Grid
section.notes: API returns wrapped object {slots:[...]}; client.ts unwraps.

section.name: System Terminal
section.notes: Hardcoded simulation logs. No API data. Terminal log entries with 5 types (INFO, WARN, ERROR, DEBUG, SYSTEM).

section.name: Architecture
section.data: Hardcoded 5-layer diagram with gRPC and event bus connections.

section.name: Footer
section.notes: Static content. Links point to "#" — need real URLs.

data_flow.step: Polls /api/v1/health every 8s. Uses local `let cancelled` flag (NOT useRef — fixed to survive StrictMode double-invoke).

step.action: Health succeeds → POST /api/v1/auth/login with JSON {email, password}

data_flow.step: Sets _jwt module variable. Then calls setStatus('ready') → backendReady=true.

step.action: backendReady flips to true → all hooks fire tryFetch()

data_flow.step: Each hook calls its fetcher (with _jwt in Authorization header). If 200: data source flips to 'live'. If fails: stays on fallback.
Renders fallback data immediately on mount. Swaps to live data when source='live'. Shows "LIVE" badge when isLive=true.

bug.symptom: Login succeeds (200, JWT received) but `backendReady` never flips to `true`. Status stuck at "warming". Warmup overlay shows "Authenticating..." indefinitely.
bug.root_cause: `cancelledRef` was a `useRef` shared between React 18 StrictMode double-invocations. First mount's effect cleanup set `cancelledRef.current = true`, which persisted into the second mount. When login completed, `if (!cancelledRef.current)` was false, so status was never updated. Function returned without setting `ready`.
bug.fix: Replaced `cancelledRef` with local `let cancelled` variable captured in closure. Each StrictMode invocation gets its own `cancelled`. Cleanup sets `cancelled = true` on its own invocation without affecting the other.

bug.symptom: Infinite re-render loop. Components re-mount endlessly.
bug.root_cause: `useCallback([fetcher])` — when components passed inline arrow functions as fetcher (e.g., `() => fetchLots()`), the fetcher reference changed every render, which caused useCallback to re-create the callback, which triggered useEffect, which triggered re-render.
bug.fix: Use `useRef` pattern: store fetcher in ref, read `fetcherRef.current()` in callback. useCallback has `[]` deps and never changes.

bug.root_cause: Sending `{username, password}` as form-encoded or JSON. API expects `{email, password}` as JSON.

bug.symptom: TypeError: records.map is not a function in PredictionEngine.
bug.root_cause: API returns `{lot_id, name, current_occupancy, current_price, records: [...]}`. Type and component expected a flat `OccupancyRecord[]`.
bug.fix: Unwrap: `const res = await fetchJson(...); return res.records`.

bug.root_cause: `r.occupied_slots / r.total_slots * 100` — these fields don't exist in API response. API returns `occupancy_rate` directly.

bug.symptom: status.valid is undefined. UI shows "undefined" in badge.
TypeError: zones.reduce is not a function in RevenueIntelligence.
bug.root_cause: API returns a single pricing zone object `{zone_id, base_price, price_range, currency, dynamic_pricing}`. Component expects `PricingZone[]`.
bug.fix: Wrap single zone in array: `return [{zone_id: ..., base_price: ..., current_multiplier: ..., city: ..., occupancy: ...}]`.

bug.symptom: TypeError: slots.forEach is not a function in MicroSlotGrid.
bug.root_cause: API returns `{lot_id, total_slots, available, ..., slots: [...]}`. Component expects flat `MicroSlot[]`.
bug.fix: Unwrap: `const res = await fetchJson(...); return res.slots`.

bug.symptom: dashboard.total_sessions is undefined (not used by MetricTicker due to default props, but incomplete).
bug.fix: Map missing fields from available data: `total_sessions: res.total_transactions, total_drivers: res.total_users`.

bug.root_cause: `base: './'` is for production builds (relative paths), breaks Vite dev server root resolution.

issue.problem: fetchDigitalTwinScenarios API endpoint might return a different shape than Scenario[]. Was not part of verified successful refetch batch.
issue.hint: Check if the endpoint exists and returns expected shape. Component uses fallbackScenarios from fallbackData.ts.

issue.problem: fetchMarlStatus API endpoint not verified. Component might not be mounted or uses useApi directly.
issue.hint: Search for fetchMarlStatus usage. May need similar shape mapping.
Check if any component calls this. If not, may be dead code.

issue.problem: Footer links point to "#" — GitHub, Docs, API, Status links are placeholder.
issue.hint: Replace with real URLs (e.g., GitHub repo, deployed API docs).

issue.problem: Architecture section FiveLayeredDiagram uses hardcoded static content. Could be animated or interactive.
issue.hint: Consider framer-motion or GSAP for layer animations.

issue.problem: Terminal data is hardcoded sample logs, not streamed from backend.
issue.hint: Could implement WebSocket or SSE for real event streaming from Render backend.

issue.problem: Three.js globe uses 800 particles with spatial grid bucketing. Verify performance on low-end devices (mobile).
issue.hint: IntersectionObserver removes globe when off-screen. `powerPreference: low-power` set. Antialias off.

issue.problem: Production build not tested. Build command: `npm run build`. Need to verify dist output works.
issue.hint: base='./' for production. Test with `npx serve dist` or deploy to Render static site.

issue.problem: Vite proxy bypasses .env, imports directly. Need to verify .env handling for different environments.
issue.hint: import.meta.env.VITE_API_URL fallback is '/api/v1'. In production static deploy, this should be full Render URL.

api_endpoints_inventory.endpoint: Returns array of Lot objects with current_occupancy and current_price.
Not verified. Component falls back to hardcoded data.
Not verified. Check if component uses it.

decision.what: React Context for shared backend state (WarmupContext) instead of prop drilling
decision.why: Avoids passing backendReady through 10 section components that don't need to know about each other.

decision.what: useApiWithFallback as canonical data fetching pattern
decision.why: Every component renders immediately with fallback data, background-fetches from API, swaps to live when ready. No loading spinners anywhere. User sees content instantly.

decision.what: Non-blocking warmup overlay (does NOT prevent section rendering)
decision.why: Sections mount and render underneath the overlay. Overlay dismisses when backendReady or on user click. User doesn't stare at a blank screen.

decision.what: Local `let cancelled` instead of `useRef` for cancellation in async effects
decision.why: useRef is shared between StrictMode double-invocations, causing cancellation leaks. Local variables are scoped per invocation.

decision.what: fetcherRef pattern in useApi instead of useCallback([fetcher])
decision.why: useCallback with fetcher in deps causes infinite loops when inline arrow functions are passed. Ref pattern stabilizes the callback reference.

decision.what: Client-side API shape transformation instead of updating TypeScript types
decision.why: The API is the source of truth. Client functions transform to match the expected TypeScript contract. Keeps components clean and types accurate.

steps.step: 1. Load page at http://localhost:5173 (or deployed URL)
2. Verify content renders immediately (no blank screen)
3. Verify warmup overlay appears showing connection progress
4. After login succeeds, overlay dismisses and "SIMULATION" badges swap to "LIVE" badges
5. Verify all 6 sections show LIVE badge (Hero, Prediction, Revenue, Blockchain, Slots, Terminal)
1. If backend is cold (e.g., after inactivity), verify app still renders with fallback data
2. Verify warmup overlay polls health endpoint every 8s
3. Verify overlay shows elapsed time and attempt count
4. Verify user can dismiss overlay manually (click) and fallback data is consistent
1. Once LIVE, verify Prediction chart shows real occupancy_rate values (not zero/NaN)
2. Verify Blockchain shows chain_length and valid status
3. Verify MicroSlot grid shows actual slot states
4. Verify Hero stat pills show computed values (lot count, total slots, revenue)
5. Verify Pricing heatmap renders without errors

critical_constraints.constraint: DO NOT change ThreeGlobe.tsx particle count or spatial grid algorithm unless you profile first. 800 particles + spatial bucketing was carefully tuned to avoid GPU thrashing on integrated GPUs.
DO NOT remove React StrictMode from main.tsx — it catches bugs we want to surface in dev.
DO NOT modify fallbackData.ts structure without updating every component that imports it — all useApiWithFallback calls depend on the shape matching.
Vite base path MUST be `'./'` in production (static deploy) and `'/'` in dev. The vite.config.ts handles this with process.env.NODE_ENV check.
Backend free tier Render spins down after 15min of inactivity. First request after idle triggers cold start (30s–10min). The warmup layer was designed for this.
